title: Bookbot Outcomes, Repository Baseline, and Evidence · Language Clinic: Definition Done
summary: A volunteer digital library asks for a word-count script but has not defined the report user, authorized input, counting policy, failure behavior, repository state, or acceptance evidence. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the definition done increment in Bookbot outcome, corpus, repository, and evidence chart…
objectives: Translate stakeholder needs into runnable behavior, changed cases, error cases, report examples, and acceptance gates.
Inspect Git status, branch, remotes, configuration origin, tracked files, ignore rules, and baseline tests before editing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Predict Definition Done · Theory Definition Done
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Bookbot team handles case 5689 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the Bookbot outcome on user need, corpus authority, repository state, and changed-case evidence; keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-definition-done — Translate stakeholder needs into runnable behavior, changed cases, error cases, report examples, and acceptance gates.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-outcomes-repo-evidence-3
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-outcomes-repo-evidence-3.
# Case 5798: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def worked_bookbot_outcomes_repo_evidence_3(user: str = "archive-reader", authorized: bool = True, acceptance_cases: int = 6, repository_clean: bool = True, changed_case_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "policy3dekrow",
        "revision5798",
        "commandworked35798",
        "report3dekrow5798",
        "test5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if not user.strip() or acceptance_cases < 1:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "corpus-authority"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not changed_case_kept:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "evidence-gap"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 1
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-charter"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Bookbot decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected file, Unicode, stream, CLI, test, package, or release case, then state which host filesystem, terminal, subprocess, Git, install, native profile, load, security, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

# Evidence: bookbot-definition-done-trf7gh

        "corpustrf7gh",
        "policyhg7frt",
        "revision2447",
        "commandtrf7gh2447",

    if not repository_clean:

    if not changed_case_kept:

    assert acceptance_cases >= 1

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Translate stakeholder needs into runnable behavior, changed cases, error cases, report examples, and acceptance gates.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Definition Done model · Theory Definition Done, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Definition Done precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Definition Done model · Theory Definition Done, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 3788 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the Bookbot outcome on user need, corpus authority, repository state, and changed-case evidence; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Translate stakeholder needs into runnable behavior, changed cases, error cases, report examples, and acceptance gates.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Definition Done model · Theory Definition Done, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A task is done when its happy-path output looks plausible.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-definition-done-trf7gh.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-definition-done-trf7gh(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_definition_done_trf7gh\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not user\.strip)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not authorized)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not repository_clean)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not changed_case_kept)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
